mnemonics.title: Register Banks Selection
mnemonics.content: RS1 RS0: 00=Bank0, 01=Bank1, 10=Bank2, 11=Bank3. Think: 'RS1,RS0 = Binary to Bank Number' - 00→0, 01→1, 10→2, 11→3

mnemonics.title: Caller vs Callee Saved
mnemonics.content: CALLEE saves what it needs, CALLER saves what it wants to keep. Callee-saved = preserved across call (SP, BP, SB, DB). Caller-saved = can be clobbered (AX, DX, temp registers).

mnemonics.title: 8051 Register Memory Map
mnemonics.content: 00-1F = Register Banks + Bit-addressable area, 20-2F = Bit register area, 30-7F = General RAM. Banks: 00-07 (Bank0), 08-0F (Bank1), 10-17 (Bank2), 18-1F (Bank3).

mnemonics.title: Live Variable Analysis
mnemonics.content: Live = 'L'ook 'I'nto 'V'ariables 'E'ventually 'L'ikely to be 'I' used 'V'aluably 'E'verywhere. If a variable will be read before written, it's LIVE.

acronyms.usage: Registers controlling hardware peripherals like timers, UART, ADC in microcontrollers
Flag register in 8051 storing carry, auxiliary carry, overflow, parity, and register bank select bits
Points to top of stack, used for pushing/popping registers and local variable storage
Holds address of next instruction to execute; modified by jumps, calls, and interrupts
16-bit register for external data memory access and lookup table addressing in 8051
Stores return address during function calls in ARM processors
Holds currently executing instruction fetched from memory

visualTips.visualization: Draw 4 boxes (banks) labeled 0-3, each containing R0-R7. Show PSW as a switch with 2 bits controlling which box is 'connected' to the R0-R7 names.
Timeline showing variable lifetimes as horizontal bars. Bars that overlap cannot share a register. Where gaps exist, register can be reused.
Stack growing downward. Draw: [Return Addr][Saved Registers][Local Vars][Params]. SP points below last item. BP provides frame reference.
Circle nodes = variables. Lines connecting nodes = overlapping lifetimes. Same-color nodes (colors = registers) cannot have connecting lines.

analogies.analogy: Like assigning seats to people in a small car. Each person (variable) needs a seat (register). If more people than seats, some must stand (spill to stack).
Think of a book you're currently reading - it's 'live'. Once you finish and close it, it's 'dead'. Future use = live, only past use = dead.
When a car's 4 seats are full and 5th person needs to ride, one person must sit in the uncomfortable back seat (stack) to make room.
Caller-saved = 'Bring your own lunch' (save your food). Callee-saved = 'The restaurant keeps tables reserved' (function preserves tables/seats).
